name: Issue Tree
description: Decompose a question into mutually exclusive, collectively exhaustive sub-questions and hypotheses
systemPromptHint: Every level must be MECE: children must not overlap (mutually exclusive) and must fully cover the parent (collectively exhaustive). If you can't confirm both, the decomposition is incomplete. Leaves are testable hypotheses, not conclusions — phrase them so data can confirm or eliminate them. Decompose questions into sub-questions, not causes into effects — this is not a causal diagram. Stop splitting when you reach a level where each branch can be investigated directly.

nodeTags.name: Question
nodeTags.description: An open question or sub-question to be decomposed further

nodeTags.name: Hypothesis
nodeTags.description: A testable explanation or answer to investigate

derivedIndicators.name: Branch
derivedIndicators.description: An intermediate decomposition — neither the root question nor a leaf hypothesis

derivedIndicators.name: Testable
derivedIndicators.description: A leaf node — should be directly investigable with data or evidence
